subject: Geography
title: OpenStreetMap and Crowdsourcing
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on OpenStreetMap and Crowdsourcing.

hook.text: OpenStreetMap is the Wikipedia of maps—built by volunteers and free for anyone to use.

buildup.text: Over two million mappers contribute roads, buildings, and trails worldwide.

discovery.text: After the 2010 Haiti earthquake, volunteers mapped Port-au-Prince in days to guide aid workers.

twist.text: In many developing countries, OpenStreetMap is more detailed than any commercial alternative.

climax.text: Humanitarian mappers now pre-map disaster-prone areas before crises strike.

punchline.text: Volunteers with laptops are mapping places no company will.

quiz.question: What is OpenStreetMap?
options: A free, volunteer-built map of the world
